title: Seer Forecast (Lock C)
description: The prediction itself. Captures the forecast statement, scenarios, confidence with provenance, falsification criteria, monitoring triggers, and baseline contrast. This is Lock C — the final immutable prediction.

statement.description: The forecast in plain language. Must be specific enough to score. E.g., 'Tesla will lose global EV market share leadership to BYD within 18 months, driven by structural bottleneck in R-channel (regulatory relationships) and field-level misfit in non-US markets.'

description.description: What this scenario looks like. Specific enough to distinguish from other scenarios at resolution.

probability.description: Assigned probability. All scenario probabilities must sum to 1.0.

structural_driver.description: The Genesis mechanism driving this scenario. E.g., 'R-channel bottleneck deepens under regulatory pressure' or 'Navigator correction (lambda recovery) stabilizes margin trajectory'.

confidence_band.description: Overall confidence in the primary forecast. E.g., '60-70%' or 'Moderate (55-65%)'.

evidence_strength.description: How strong is the evidence base? Pushes confidence up or down.

structural_clarity.description: How clear is the structural diagnosis? Is the bottleneck unambiguous?

proxy_dependence.description: How much does the forecast rely on proxy evidence? Higher proxy dependence widens uncertainty.

shock_sensitivity.description: How vulnerable is this forecast to exogenous shocks?

challenge_impact.description: What challenges were raised during the diagnostic, and how did they affect confidence?

falsification_criteria.description: Specific observable outcomes that would prove the forecast wrong. These are the scientific backbone — without them, the forecast isn't falsifiable.

monitoring_triggers.description: Events or signals that should prompt a check-in or status update on a live forecast.

divergence.description: Where Seer disagrees with the baseline and why.

hinge.description: The specific structural factor that will determine who is right — Seer or the baseline.

bottleneck_channel.description: The SIRF channel constraining capacity. E.g., 'R (Relational)'.

regime.description: Current work mode / deferral regime. E.g., 'Resistance (D_r) — defending current configuration'.

margin_posture.description: Margin trajectory assessment. E.g., 'dM/dt < 0, volitional window narrowing'.

field_fit.description: Channel-specific field-fit assessment. E.g., 'chi_S high, chi_R low in EU regulatory field'.

navigation_profile.description: Navigator classification. E.g., 'Visionary quadrant — high m, low lambda'.
